title: Behavioral Contract Schema
description: Schema for validating npm package behavioral contract files. As of v3, every profile carries a coverage_status. 'covered' (default, omitted in legacy profiles) means the profile enforces postconditions. 'no-async-contract' means the package was researched and confirmed to have no async error contract worth enforcing (e.g. UI primitives, presentational libs).

package.description: The npm package name this contract applies to

semver.description: Semver range this contract applies to

contract_version.description: Semver version of this contract file itself

maintainer.description: GitHub username or team maintaining this contract

last_verified.description: ISO date when this contract was last verified against package documentation

status.description: Quality and validation status of this contract

deprecated_date.description: Date when this contract was deprecated

evidence_quality.description: Quality level of the evidence backing this contract's claims. Required when coverage_status is 'covered' (or omitted, which defaults to 'covered').

coverage_status.description: Profile coverage state. 'covered' (default) = profile has postconditions to enforce against caller code. 'no-async-contract' = package researched, async surface doesn't fit our error-contract model (UI primitives, presentational libs, utility libs, result-object-pattern wrappers like neverthrow). 'out-of-scope' = package researched, doesn't fit our ICP (build tools, IDE tooling, low-callsite framework internals, low-real-world-usage). 'deprecated' = package is EOL or self-deprecated in npm metadata. The three non-'covered' values are stub profiles — they document the research outcome so coverage reporting and bc-version-drift can distinguish 'researched and cleared' from 'never analyzed'. Drift watches stubs for major version bumps that might introduce a contractable async surface.

skip_reason.description: Fine-grained category for non-'covered' coverage_status values. Preserves the per-gate skip taxonomy from bc-npm-package-onboard Phase 0 (UI primitive, utility, low-callsite, build-tool, EOL, etc.) inside the corpus stub itself. Optional but recommended for stub profiles. Maps from state.json's `skipped-<reason>` status values.

researched_at.description: ISO date when the package was researched to determine its coverage_status. Required for all non-'covered' (stub) profiles.

coverage_rationale.description: Human-readable explanation of why the package has the assigned coverage_status. Required for all non-'covered' (stub) profiles — explains what was reviewed (e.g. 'all exports are React components with no async I/O, no thrown errors, no error contracts to enforce'). Optional for covered profiles.

extends.description: Relative path (from this contract.yaml's directory) to a parent contract.yaml to inherit from. Child overrides parent's fields where present; functions deep-merge by name, postconditions/preconditions/edge_cases merge by id. Use to fork a version-specific profile when most postconditions are unchanged from the parent.

functions.description: List of functions covered by this contract. Required when coverage_status is 'covered' (or omitted). May be omitted or empty when coverage_status is 'no-async-contract'.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this function does

preconditions.description: Conditions that must be true before calling this function

postconditions.description: What happens after calling this function (returns/throws)

business_impact.description: Business impact of a violation of this rule — used to surface counterfactual value to users

production_behavior.description: How this violation manifests at runtime

visibility.description: Whether the failure is observable by the developer or user

user_experience.description: User-facing impact when this violation triggers in production

incident_cost_range.description: Typical cost of a production incident caused by this violation: low <$100, medium $100-$10k, high $10k-$100k, critical >$100k

incident_label.description: Primary business risk category shown to users in place of ERROR/WARNING

id.description: Unique identifier for this precondition within the function

description.description: What must be true before calling the function

source.description: Deprecated: use sources array instead. Kept for backward compatibility.

sources.description: URLs to authoritative documentation supporting this claim

id.description: Unique identifier for this postcondition within the function

throws.description: What error type is thrown (if applicable)

required_handling.description: What the caller MUST do to handle this case

source.description: Deprecated: use sources array instead. Kept for backward compatibility.

sources.description: URLs to authoritative documentation supporting this claim

id.description: Unique identifier for this edge case within the function

source.description: Deprecated: use sources array instead. Kept for backward compatibility.

sources.description: URLs to documentation or GitHub issues describing this edge case

severity.description: Edge cases cannot have error severity
